description: Tier 2 AI nuance bonus eval suite. The bonus reads free-text bios for signal Tier 1 structured scoring missed, rewarding specificity/evidence over ritual keywords (ADR-001 amendment 2026-06-12). Adjustment is hard-capped at [-10, 10] in code. Bonus floors at 0 for 'nothing notable'; negative is reserved for genuine revealed friction (decision 2026-06-12). Cases assert direction + groundedness, NOT exact scores (LLM judgments vary run-to-run). Each case runs N=3 times; consistency = max-min adjustment must be <= max_spread (ADR-002).

direction_values.neutral_or_zero: adjustment approximately 0; small magnitude either side acceptable
direction_values.mild_positive: adjustment >= 0 and small; positive allowed, must not be strongly negative

cases.description: Structured data may pass Tier 1, but bios reveal a quiet-vs-social lifestyle clash the 1-5 scales flatten.
cases.bio_a: Home is my safe space. I value peace, quiet, and a comfortable environment where I can recharge after a long day.
cases.bio_b: Being surrounded by friends and like-minded people is what makes a place feel like home. I enjoy hosting friends, socializing, and spending time with roommates.

expected.note: Neutral under Option B (new-signal-only): quiet-vs-social is a restated noise/guests-slider preference already scored by Tier 1, NOT new text-only signal -> bonus 0. must_reference emptied to match the neutral label, same as ai-003: a 'nothing new' rationale explains the absence (talks about the sliders) and need not re-cite bio specifics.

cases.description: Both bios recite generic virtues (clean, responsible, friendly) with no specific behavior, evidence, or track record. Tier 1 already credited any real compatibility; the bonus must not inflate on warm vocabulary alone.

must_not_reference: specific habits or track record not present in the bios
expected.note: A ~0 bonus means 'bios added nothing new' - NOT a bad match. Guards against double-counting what Tier 1 scored.

cases.description: PRECONDITION: this pair clears the Tier 1 smoking hard-constraint (A is a non-smoker but TOLERANT of smoking, so scoring runs). The negative bonus is about social-lifestyle friction revealed in the bios, NOT the smoking itself (which Tier 1 already handles).
cases.bio_a: Clean, responsible, non-smoker, respects privacy, and will take great care of the apartment.
cases.bio_b: Part of the 420 community. I occasionally host friends, sometimes including neighbors, but always keep the space tidy.

must_not_reference: a hard smoking rejection (that is Tier 1's job)
expected.note: Neutral under Option B: A's privacy claim and B's occasional hosting restate the noise/guests sliders -> no new signal. must_reference emptied to match the neutral label (a 'nothing new' rationale explains an absence and need not cite specifics); must_not_reference still guards against re-litigating the Tier 1 smoking/420 hard constraint.

cases.description: BILATERAL evidence positive: BOTH bios carry concrete, verifiable track records of running a shared cleaning system with a past roommate, plus specific habits beyond generic claims. The positive is about the pair (two demonstrated coordinators), not one-directional - which is what lets the cheap model score it without escalation (ADR-005).
cases.bio_a: In my last place, my roommate and I kept a shared chore chart for two years - we reset it every Sunday and it genuinely kept the peace. I'm a big believer in agreeing on a cleaning system up front rather than letting things build up. I cook most nights, so I'm fastidious about resetting the kitchen after.
cases.bio_b: With my previous roommate we ran a shared cleaning rota for a couple of years, and I'd happily set one up again. I'm extremely tidy - I can't go to bed unless the kitchen and living room are clean - and I keep a no-shoes-inside household.

expected.note: The positive case - rewards specificity and verifiable history, the core 'substance over vocabulary' principle.

cases.description: Trap: invented clash. 'Nurse' + '12-hour shifts' tempts an inference of NIGHT shifts and a sleep conflict that the bio never states. A grounded judge does not invent it.
cases.bio_a: I'm a nurse and work three 12-hour shifts a week, so my schedule's a bit unusual. The other days I'm home a lot and like keeping things calm.
cases.bio_b: Quiet, tidy, keep to myself mostly.

expected.note: Tests that the LLM does not fabricate a clash from a plausible-but-unstated inference.

cases.description: Trap: invented blocker. Shared love of animals leans mild-positive (absence of a stated allergy is itself meaningful - people flag allergies prominently). The only hard floor: the rationale must not assert an INVENTED blocker (a fabricated allergy or lease ban not in the bios).
cases.bio_a: My golden retriever Biscuit is the love of my life and comes everywhere with me.
cases.bio_b: I love animals and grew up with dogs.

must_not_reference: a lease/landlord pet ban not stated
B refusing to cohabit with the dog
expected.note: Distinguishes a reasonable inference from absence (grounded) from inventing a fact (hallucination). Mild positive is correct.

cases.description: Trap: profession stereotype. 'Finance job' tempts inferences of long hours, high stress, type-A personality - none stated. A grounded judge does not score the profession.
cases.bio_a: I just moved here for a finance job and I'm focused on settling in.
cases.bio_b: Looking for someone laid-back and drama-free.

must_not_reference: type-A or any personality trait inferred from 'finance'
expected.note: Tests that the LLM does not stereotype from a job title; insufficient bio signal means bonus ~0.

cases.description: Negative case under Option B that is NEITHER a slider value NOR a hard constraint: a conflict-style mismatch. A confronts issues head-on in the moment; B shuts down when confronted and needs a day or two to process alone. No questionnaire field asks about conflict/communication style, and it is not a binary house-rule - it is graded interpersonal friction the bonus should penalize negative.
cases.bio_a: When something's bothering me I bring it up the same day - I'd rather knock on your door and talk it through right then than let it fester. I'm direct, and I expect to clear the air fast.
cases.bio_b: I really can't deal with being confronted on the spot - it makes me shut down. If there's an issue I need a day or two to cool off and process on my own before I'm able to talk about it calmly.

expected.note: New-signal negative, neither slider nor hard constraint: a conflict/communication-style mismatch (direct/immediate vs. needs-to-retreat). No structured field captures conflict style, and it is graded interpersonal friction, not a house-rule the hard-constraint layer would catch.
